documents.first_three_pages_text:
An Exact Spectral Obstruction to Froggatt–Nielsen Ladders
from the Octonion Associator
Abstract
In octonionic approaches to flavor, a natural mass operator on a triple of generation
vectorsu 1, u2, u3 ∈Im(O) is the associator Gram matrixM ab =∥[u a, ub, H]∥2 against a fixed
vacuum directionH. Numerical scans of this operator found that its normalized singular
spectrum forms a geometric (“Froggatt–Nielsen”) ladder (1, x, x2) only at the golden base
x=φ −1 ≈0.618, and that Cabibbo-like bases are unreachable at any frame. We prove both
facts. Alternativity forcesMto be symmetric andhollow(zero diagonal); since its entries are
squared norms,Mis entrywise nonnegative and traceless, and the sign structure of hollow
symmetric nonnegative matrices then yields the exact sum rules 1 =s 2 +s 3 for its singular
values, at every frame and every vacuum, with no orthonormality assumption. A geometric
ladder is therefore possible only if 1 =x+x 2, whose unique positive root is the inverse golden
ratio: the appearance ofφis a theorem rather than an emergent feature, and the exclusion
of hierarchical Cabibbo textures is exact. Numerically the pattern extends to larger frames
as ak-bonacci family: forn-frames the sum rules 1 = P
i≥2 si holds on every sampled frame
— although it is not implied by matrix theory alone forn≥4 — and gradient searches
realize the geometric ladder exactly at the reciprocal tribonacci, tetranacci, and pentanacci
constants forn= 4,5,6. The program of deriving Froggatt–Nielsen hierarchies from this
operator class is thereby closed, not merely unsuccessful.
1 Setup
LetOdenote the octonions with imaginary unitse 1, . . . , e7 multiplying according to a fixed
orientation of the Fano plane, and let Im(O) ∼= R7 be the imaginary subspace [1]. The associator
[x, y, z] = (xy)z−x(yz)
measures the failure of associativity. BecauseOis analternativealgebra — the subalgebra
generated by any two elements is associative (Artin’s theorem [2]) — the associator is a to-
tally antisymmetric trilinear function of its arguments; in particular it vanishes whenever two
arguments coincide, and for octonions it is purely imaginary.
Octonionic and exceptional-algebraic structures (J 3(O),E 6) have long been proposed as
organizing frameworks for one generation of Standard Model fermions [3], with renewed recent
activity relating exceptional Jordan spectra to mass ratios and mixing [8, 9]. With the three
generations associated to a triple of directions in Im(O), a candidate flavor operator, studied
numerically in the program this note completes, is
Mab(U;H) =
 [u a, ub, H]
2, a, b∈ {1,2,3},(1)
1
Matthew Connelly
11 July 2026
whereU= (u 1, u2, u3) is a triple (“frame”) of imaginary octonions andH∈Im(O) is a fixed
vacuum direction (e.g.H=e 7). The phenomenological target is a Froggatt–Nielsen (FN) texture
[4]: a normalized singular spectrum of the geometric form
(s1, s2, s3)/s1 = (1, x, x2),
with basex=λ 2 ≈0.0503 (down-type) orx=λ 4 ≈0.0025 (up-type), whereλ≈0.2243 is the
Cabibbo parameter [5].
Two robust numerical facts emerged from scans of this operator over the Stiefel manifold
St(7,3):
1. Direct optimization toward geometric ladders converges, with residual at machine zero, to
thegolden ladder(1, φ −1, φ−2) = (1,0.618034,0.381966), whereφ= (1 +
√
5)/2. Among
8000 random frames, all 859 whose spectra were incidentally near-geometric (residual
<10 −4) had base in [0.614,0.622], median 0.61810.
2. Optimization toward anyotherprescribed base — including both Cabibbo targets — stalls
at residuals of order 10 −1.
These facts were previously reported as an empirical anomaly: the operator “prefers” the golden
ratio and “refuses” the Cabibbo hierarchy. Both are consequences of a three-step argument
requiring nothing beyond alternativity and elementary matrix theory.
2 The theorem
Lemma 1(structure ofM).For arbitraryu 1, u2, u3, H∈Im(O)— no orthonormality or
genericity assumed — the matrixM ab =∥[u a, ub, H]∥2 is symmetric, entrywise nonnegative,
and hollow (identically zero diagonal); in particulartrM= 0.
Proof.Entries are squared norms, hence nonnegative. By alternativity the associator is anti-
symmetric in its first two arguments, so [ub, ua, H] =−[ua, ub, H] and the two have equal norms:
Mis symmetric. Antisymmetry likewise gives [u a, ua, H] = 0, soMaa = 0.
Lemma 2(sum rule).LetMbe a real symmetric3×3matrix with nonnegative entries and
zero trace, and lets 1 ≥s 2 ≥s 3 ≥0be its singular values. Then
s1 =s 2 +s 3.
Proof.Letλ 1 ≥λ 2 ≥λ 3 be the eigenvalues ofM; by symmetry{s i}={|λ i|}. BecauseMis
entrywise nonnegative, the Perron–Frobenius theorem givesλ 1 =ρ(M)≥ |λ i|for alli, with
λ1 ≥0. Supposeλ 2 >0. Thenλ 1 +λ 2 +λ 3 = 0 gives|λ 3|=λ 1 +λ 2 > λ1 =ρ(M), a
contradiction. Henceλ 2, λ3 ≤0 ands 2 +s 3 =−λ 2 −λ 3 =λ 1 =s 1.
Remark 1.The sign structure in Lemma 2 is a special case of known results on hollow symmet-
ric nonnegative matrices: Charles, Farber, Johnson, and Kennedy-Shaffer [6] show that ann×n
hollow symmetric nonnegative matrix has between2andn−1nonpositive eigenvalues, which
forn= 3forces exactly two. We include the elementary proof to keep the note self-contained;
the contribution here is the octonionic application, not the matrix fact.
2
Theorem 1(golden rigidity).For every frameUand vacuumH, the singular values ofM(U;H)
satisfys 1 =s 2 +s 3. Consequently, if the normalized spectrum is a geometric ladder(1, x, x 2)
withx >0, then
1 =x+x 2 =⇒x=
√
5−1
2 =φ −1,
and the ladder is(1, φ −1, φ−2): the defining equation of the golden ratio is imposed by the
operator’s structure.
Explicit form andG2-covariance.For algebraic work the operator should be written out
in structure constants. Withϕ ijk the Fano 3-form, Φ = 1
6 εϕits Hodge dual, andA ijml =
[ei, ej, em]l =−2Φ ijml, trilinearity gives [u a, ub, H]l = P ui
auj
bH mAijml, soMis an explicit
quadratic form in the vacuum:

(2)
with eachQ (ab) positive semidefinite andKprecomputed in the reference implementation
(octonion.py::build K tensor). SinceG 2 = Aut(O) preservesϕ(hence Φ,A), covariance is
manifest:M(gU;gH) =M(U;H) for allg∈G 2, with residual symmetry Stab G2(H) ∼= SU(3)
onceHis fixed. An algebraic attack on Conjecture 1 should proceed through the standard
ε–ϕ–Φ contraction identities applied toK.
Uniqueness is one-directional.Theorem 1 makes the golden spectrum the uniquegeometric
spectrum, not the unique spectrum: on the sum-rule surface the normalized spectra (1, r,1−r)
form a one-parameter family, and the original random scan realizedr=s 2/s1 across essentially
its full admissible range [0.5006,0.9994]. The theorem collapses the geometric locus to a point;
it does not collapse the operator’s spectral range.
Corollary 1(exact FN obstruction).No frame and no vacuum realize a geometric ladder
at any basex̸=φ −1. In particular the Cabibbo textures are excluded exactly: forx=λ 2,
x+x 2 ≈0.053̸= 1; forx=λ 4,x+x 2 ≈0.0025̸= 1. More generally, any target spectrum with
s2 +s 3 ̸=s 1 — which includes every strongly hierarchical texture(1, ϵ, ϵ′)withϵ+ϵ ′ <1— is
unrealizable by this operator class at any point of its domain.
The sum rule is also the operator’s positive prediction: any model in which physical mass pa-
rameters are identified (in some normalization) with the singular values of an octonion-associator
Gram matrix of this type predicts the exact linear relationm 1 =m 2 +m 3 among the three nor-
malized values. No observed fermion mass or Yukawa hierarchy satisfies such a relation, which
is an independent phenomenological statement of the obstruction.
3 Thek-bonacci family: larger frames
For ann-frameu 1, . . . , un ∈Im(O) (n≤7) the same construction gives a hollow symmetric
nonnegativen×nmatrix, but forn≥4 the matrix facts alone no longer force the sum rule: by
[6], hollow symmetric nonnegative matrices with only two nonpositive eigenvalues exist for every
n≥4, and for such matricess 1 < P
i≥2 si fails. Whether the sum rule survives is therefore a
question about theimageof the octonionic map (1), not about hollow matrices in general.
3

documents.first_three_pages_text:
Octonionic Flavor Geometry: Spectral Rigidity,
the n = 7 Boundary, and a CKM-Localizing
Exceptional EFT
Matthew Connelly
13 July 2026
Abstract
We consolidate a sequence of results on octonionic flavor geometry. The associator
operator Mab =∥[ua,u b,H ]∥2 reduces, after fixing the vacuum direction, to a complexSU(3)
cross-product norm. This makesM conditionally negative definite and proves a universal
singular-value sum rule: for every nonzero finite frame,s1 =∑
i>1si. For three generations, a
geometric spectrum (1,x,x 2) is therefore possible only atx =φ−1, excluding Cabibbo-scaled
Froggatt–Nielsen ladders exactly rather than approximately. Numerical searches realize the
associated k-bonacci ladders throughn = 6, while the fulln = 7 frame repeatedly stalls at a
first-order obstruction. At saturation the transverse complex Gram matrix obeysG2 = 2G
and rankG = 3, indicating a real-algebraic obstruction rather than an elementary topological
one; a formal non-realizability certificate remains open.
The flavor program is then lifted from singular-value ladders to the exceptional Jordan
algebra J3(O) and its Freudenthal triple system. Tested even invariant actions collapse to a
2+1 mixing block. A chiral pseudoscalar supplies the orientation-sensitive torque required for
heavy–light leakage, with canonical coefficient1/2. A compact Freudenthal moment map and
complexτ-real Jordan-slot mixing reproduce CKM magnitudes and CP violation to numerical
precision, localizing the missing structure in two complex coefficientsr and s. However,
scalar slots are inert for the compact generation projection, and the direct invariant actions
and attractor closures tested here do not select the fitted coefficients. The defensible endpoint
is therefore a rigid exceptional-geometric core plus an external Higgs/Yukawa wedge that
selects the CKM-localizing spurion invariants. This is a claim-controlled effective-field-theory
framework, not a completed zero-parameter derivation of Standard Model flavor.
Keywords: octonions; non-associativity; Froggatt–Nielsen hierarchies;k-bonacci ladders; ex-
ceptional Jordan algebra; Freudenthal triple system; CKM matrix; chiral pseudoscalar; effective
field theory.
1 Introduction
Octonionic and exceptional-algebraic structures have long been considered as organizing frame-
works for fermions and generation structure [1, 3, 10]. The specific question addressed here
is narrower and testable: can octonionic non-associativity generate the observed hierarchical
pattern of quark masses and mixings rather than merely accommodate it?
The natural first candidate is an associator-derived mass operator. Numerical searches did not
approach Cabibbo-like Froggatt–Nielsen (FN) ladders [4, 5]; instead, they repeatedly converged
to the inverse golden ratio. The apparent numerical anomaly can be upgraded to an exact
theorem. That theorem closes the direct FN-ladder program for this operator, while identifying
a rigid positive structure worth retaining.
1
The second part of the paper asks what survives after that obstruction. We retain octonionic
geometry as shared kinematic data but extract physical generation observables through the
exceptional Jordan algebraJ3(O) and a compact Freudenthal moment map. This larger setting
localizes CKM mixing and the Jarlskog phase [6, 7, 8], but it also exposes a clear dynamical
boundary: the direct zero-parameter selectors tested so far do not choose the CKM-localizing
point.
Table 1 states the epistemic status of the main claims. This distinction is load-bearing:
analytic theorems, machine-precision realizations, numerical obstruction evidence, diagnostic
fits, and negative tests of specified closures are not interchangeable.
Table 1: Claim-status ledger.
Claim Status Boundary
SU(3) associator reduction Analytic The octonion convention must be stated.
Universal CND sum rule Analytic theorem Does not prove that everyk-bonacci ladder
is attained.
Golden three-rung rigidity Analytic theorem Unique geometric spectrum, not unique
spectrum overall.
n = 4, 5, 6 ladders Machine-precision
realizations
Formal attainment proofs remain open.
n = 7 hexanacci failure Strong numerical
obstruction
No formal emptiness certificate yet.
Chiral coefficient 1/2 Analytic normalization Does not select CKM by itself.
Compact FTS moment map Verified implementation Not equivalent to a projected3×3 shortcut.
CKM-localizing r,s Four-real-parameter
diagnostic fit
Not a zero-parameter prediction.
Direct attractor closures Negative computational
gates
Only the specified closures are ruled out.
Higgs wedge Phenomenological
selector
Not a UV completion.
2 The associator mass operator
2.1 Definition and non-associative content
Let O be the octonions, letIm(O)≃ R7 be their imaginary subspace, and define the associator
by
[x,y,z ] = (xy)z−x(yz). (1)
For frame vectorsu1,...,u n∈ Im(O) and a vacuum directionH∈ Im(O), define
Mab(U;H) =∥[ua,ub,H ]∥2. (2)
The octonions are alternative: every subalgebra generated by two elements is associative
[2]. The associator is therefore totally antisymmetric. It follows immediately thatM is real,
symmetric, entrywise nonnegative, and hollow:
Mab =Mba≥ 0, M aa = 0, TrM = 0. (3)
The vanishing diagonal is an algebraic consequence of alternativity, not a fitted subtraction.
Genuine non-associativity is what permits the off-diagonal entries to be nonzero.
2
2.2 Fixed-vacuum SU(3) reduction
Using G2 = Aut(O) covariance, fixH =e7. The residual stabilizer isSU(3) and
Im(O)≃ R⊕ C3. (4)
Writeua = xae7 +za with za∈ C3. The singlet part drops out of the associator. In a fixed
Cayley–Dickson/Fano convention one obtains, up to convention-dependent conjugations,
[za,zb,e 7]∼ 2i (za×zb), M ab = 4∥za×zb∥2. (5)
Let ra =∥za∥2 and Gab =⟨za,zb⟩ be the complex Gram matrix. The complex Lagrange identity
gives
Mab = 4
(
rarb−|Gab|2
)
. (6)
Equation (6) identifies the operator as a misalignment energy: parallel transverse directions
generate no associator mass, whereas complex area, or twist, generates a positive pairwise entry.
2.3 Conditional negative definiteness
Let Z be the 3× n complex matrix whose columns are za. For a real coefficient vector
c = (c1,...,c n) define
A =Z diag(ca)Z†. (7)
A direct calculation from Eq. (6) yields
1
4cTMc = (TrA)2− Tr(A2). (8)
On the codimension-one hyperplane∑
acara = 0, one hasTrA = 0. SinceA is Hermitian,
cTMc =−4 Tr(A2)≤ 0. (9)
ThusM is conditionally negative definite (CND).
Theorem 2.1(Universal sum rule). For any finite collectionu1,...,u n,H ∈ Im(O), the matrix
in Eq.(2) has at most one positive eigenvalue. IfM̸= 0, its ordered singular values satisfy
s1 =
n∑
i=2
si. (10)
If M = 0, the identity is trivial.
Proof. Conditional negative definiteness implies thatM has at most one positive eigenvalue.
By Eq.(3), M is traceless. IfM is nonzero, trace zero forces exactly one positive eigenvalue,
whose magnitude equals the sum of the magnitudes of all negative eigenvalues. BecauseM is
symmetric, its singular values are the absolute values of its eigenvalues, proving Eq. (10).
Forn = 3, the same sign conclusion can also be obtained directly from the Perron–Frobenius
theorem and the known inertia restrictions on hollow, symmetric, nonnegative matrices [9]. The
CND derivation is stronger because it exposes the octonionic image responsible for the rule at
arbitrary finite frame size.
3

documents.first_three_pages_text:
CONNELLY  |  OCTONIONIC FLAVOR GEOMETRY
Matthew Connelly  •  13 July 2026  • 
OCTONIONIC FLAVOR GEOMETRY
Spectral Rigidity, the n = 7 Boundary, and a CKM-Localizing 
Exceptional EFT
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Consolidated Research Note
Matthew Connelly
13 July 2026
Research status
This note distinguishes analytic theorems, verified numerical gates, diagnostic fits, negative results 
for tested closures, and genuinely open problems. It presents a constrained effective-field-theory 
framework—not a completed zero-parameter derivation of Standard Model flavor.
CONNELLY  |  OCTONIONIC FLAVOR GEOMETRY
Matthew Connelly  •  13 July 2026  • 
Abstract
This note consolidates a sequence of results on octonionic flavor geometry. The basic associator 
operator M_ab = ||[u_a,u_b,H]||² is reduced, after fixing the vacuum, to a complex SU(3) cross-
product norm. This makes the matrix conditionally negative definite and proves a universal 
singular-value sum rule: for any nonzero finite frame, s₁ = Σᵢ>₁ sᵢ. In the three-generation case, a 
geometric spectrum (1,x,x²) is therefore possible only at x = φ⁻¹, excluding Cabibbo-scaled Froggatt–
Nielsen ladders exactly rather than approximately. Numerical searches realize the corresponding k-
bonacci ladders through n = 6, while n = 7 stalls at a reproducible first-order obstruction. At full 
frame size the transverse complex Gram matrix obeys the tight-frame identity G² = 2G with rank(G) 
= 3, indicating that the n = 7 failure is real-algebraic rather than an elementary topological 
obstruction; a formal non-realizability certificate remains open.
The flavor program then shifts from singular-value ladders to the exceptional Jordan algebra J₃(O) 
and its Freudenthal triple system. Even invariant actions collapse to a 2 + 1 mixing block. A chiral 
pseudoscalar supplies the orientation-sensitive torque needed for heavy-light leakage, with 
canonical coefficient 1/2. A compact Freudenthal moment map and complex tau-real Jordan-slot 
mixing reproduce CKM magnitudes and CP violation to numerical precision, localizing the missing 
structure in two complex coefficients r and s. However, scalar slots are inert for the compact 
generation projection, and the tested direct invariant actions and attractor closures do not select the 
fitted coefficients. The defensible endpoint is thus a rigid exceptional-geometric core plus an 
external Higgs/Yukawa wedge that selects the CKM-localizing spurion invariants.
Executive findings
 Exact theorem: the associator mass matrix has at most one positive eigenvalue and satisfies s₁ = 
Σᵢ>₁ sᵢ whenever it is nonzero.
 Exact three-generation consequence: geometric singular-value ladders exist only at the inverse 
golden ratio; strongly hierarchical Froggatt–Nielsen targets lie outside the image of this operator 
class.
 Numerical extension: reciprocal tribonacci, tetranacci, and pentanacci ladders are reached at 
machine precision for n = 4, 5, 6.
 n = 7 boundary: repeated optimizations satisfy four of five ladder constraints, but the fifth aligns 
with a weak Jacobian direction; the evidence is strong, yet not a formal emptiness proof.
 CKM localization: complex interference inside shared Jordan slots reproduces the CKM matrix 
and Jarlskog invariant; phase information does not live in the scalar FTS slots.
 Dynamical limitation: the direct zero-parameter attractor route tested so far does not select the 
CKM-localizing coefficients.
One-sentence outcome
Octonionic non-associativity provides a rigid spectral law and a canonical chiral mechanism, but 
the present construction still needs a physically justified Higgs/GUT boundary principle to select 
the observed CKM point.
CONNELLY  |  OCTONIONIC FLAVOR GEOMETRY
Matthew Connelly  •  13 July 2026  • 
1. Research question and progression
The original question was whether octonionic non-associativity could generate the observed 
hierarchical pattern of quark masses and mixings rather than merely accommodate it. The natural 
first candidate was an associator-derived mass operator. That operator did not converge to Cabibbo-
like ladders; instead, it repeatedly converged to the inverse golden ratio. The apparent numerical 
anomaly was then converted into an exact theorem. The later work asks what remains useful after 
that obstruction and whether a larger exceptional-algebraic construction can carry mixing and CP 
phase data.
Stage Question Result
Associator scan Can M generate FN-like ladders? Only the golden ladder appears for n = 3.
Rigidity 
theorem
Is the golden preference 
accidental? No. Hollow positivity forces s₁ = s₂ + s₃.
Larger frames Does the rule extend beyond 
three vectors? CND proves the sum rule; k-bonacci attainment is numerical.
Full n = 7 frame Does the hexanacci ladder exist? Strong numerical obstruction under tight-frame saturation; 
proof open.
Jordan/FTS lift Can exceptional structure encode 
CKM mixing? Yes diagnostically through complex shared-slot mixing.
Attractor gate Does pure FTS dynamics select 
the CKM point? Not under the closures tested.
The progression matters: the negative mass-ladder result is not a dead end. It identifies precisely what the simplest 
operator cannot do, and it tells us what any successful extension must change.
2. Associator operator and exact spectral rigidity
2.1 Definition and non-associative content
Let O be the octonions, Im(O)  R⁷ their imaginary subspace, and [x,y,z] = (xy)z − x(yz) the ≅
associator. For frame vectors u₁,…,uₙ and vacuum direction H, define
M_ab(U;H) = ||[u_a,u_b,H]|| .²
Alternativity makes the associator totally antisymmetric. Hence M is real, symmetric, entrywise 
nonnegative, and hollow: M_aa = 0. The diagonal vanishes for an algebraic reason, not because it 
has been fitted or manually subtracted. This is the load-bearing non-associative fingerprint of the 
construction.
2.2 Fixed-vacuum SU(3) reduction
Using G₂ covariance, fix H = e₇. The imaginary octonions split as R  C³, and each u_a can be written⊕  
u_a = x_a e₇ + z_a. The singlet component x_a e₇ drops out of the associator. Up to a stated octonion 
convention,
[z_a,z_b,e ] ~ 2i (z_a × z_b),       M_ab = 4||z_a × z_b|| .₇ ²
With r_a = ||z_a||² and complex Gram matrix G_ab = z_a,z_b , the complex Lagrange identity ⟨ ⟩
gives
M_ab = 4(r_a r_b − |G_ab| ).²

Weighted Conditional Negativity and Exact Spectral
Obstructions
for Octonionic Associator Energy Matrices
Matthew Connelly
July 2026
Abstract
Let u1, . . . , un and H be imaginary octonions and define the pairwise associator-energy
matrix
Mab = ∥[ua, ub, H]∥2, [x, y, z] = ( xy)z − x(yz).
After fixing a nonzero vacuum direction, the octonion associator reduces to a complex SU (3)
cross product. On the active support this gives
Mab = 4
�
rarb − |Gab|2
,
where G is a complex Gram matrix and ra = Gaa. We prove that the diagonally normalized
matrix is conditionally negative definite in the standard sense and that the raw matrix M ,
by congruence, has at most one positive eigenvalue. Since M is hollow, every nonzero such
matrix has exactly one positive eigenvalue and its ordered singular values obey the universal
identity
s1 =
nX
j=2
sj.
Consequently, an exact geometric spectrum (1, x, . . . , xn−1) can occur only when 1 = x+· · ·+
xn−1. For n = 3 the unique positive base is the inverse golden ratio. We also derive the exact
least-squares obstruction to any prescribed three-rung base and show that archived numerical
floors coincide with the analytic formula. The result excludes strongly hierarchical geometric
singular spectra for this operator class; it does not exclude Froggatt–Nielsen models generally,
other octonionic operators, or nonlinear maps from the spectrum to physical masses. For
complete orthonormal seven-frames we record additional tight-frame identities, while leaving
exact hexanacci nonattainability as an open algebraic problem.
1 Introduction
Octonionic and exceptional-algebraic structures have long been investigated as organizing frame-
works for fermionic degrees of freedom and generation structure [ 3, 1]. A particularly direct
construction assigns to generation directions ua ∈ Im(O) and a vacuum direction H ∈ Im(O)
the symmetric matrix
Mab(U ; H) = ∥[ua, ub, H]∥2. (1)
Because octonions are alternative, the associator is alternating. Thus M is entrywise nonnega-
tive and hollow. It is nevertheless not a Gram matrix in the usual positive-semidefinite sense;
throughout this paper we call it the associator-energy matrix.
A natural spectral question is whether the ordered singular values of ( 1) can form a geometric
ladder
(s1, . . . , sn)
s1
= (1, x, x2, . . . , xn−1), 0 < x < 1. (2)
1
Small choices such as x ∼ λ2 or x ∼ λ4, with λ the Cabibbo parameter, resemble the hier-
archical powers used in Froggatt–Nielsen model building [ 6, 7]. Equation ( 2), however, is a
specific singular-value ansatz for the operator ( 1); it is not a definition of the Froggatt–Nielsen
mechanism.
The central observation is that ( 1) has Lorentzian-type inertia for every finite collection of
vectors, with no orthonormality assumption. The strongest statements and their boundaries
are summarized in Table 1.
Claim Status Boundary
SU (3) cross-product reduction analytic, convention
fixed
a different octonion convention
changes phases and signs, not the
norm identity
At most one positive eigenvalue
and s1 =P
j>1 sj
analytic theorem applies to the operator ( 1), not to
arbitrary octonionic mass operators
Unique admissible geometric base analytic necessary
condition
attainability under extra frame
constraints is separate
Three-rung residual floor analytic theorem the archived optimization uses the
squared Euclidean residual defined
below
Archived frame and matrix checks verified numerical
audit
internal and operator-level
consistency, not a replacement for
the proof
Full seven-frame hexanacci miss numerical
obstruction evidence
no formal emptiness certificate is
claimed
Table 1: Claim-status register.
2 Octonionic reduction at fixed vacuum
2.1 Conventions
Let O denote the real octonions in the Cayley–Dickson convention O = H ⊕ Hℓ, with multipli-
cation inherited from
(a, b)(c, d) =
�
ac − d b, da + bc

.
Write 1, e1, . . . , e7 for the resulting orthonormal basis and identify Im(O) with R7. The norm is
multiplicative and the algebra is alternative; in particular the associator is a totally alternating
trilinear map [ 2, 1].
For a nonzero H, the transitive action of G2 = Aut( O) on the unit sphere in Im(O) allows
us to rotate H/∥H∥ to e7. Trilinearity then contributes only an overall factor ∥H∥2 to M . The
case H = 0 is trivial.
In the fixed convention, define

3 Weighted conditional negativity and inertia
The phrase “conditionally negative definite” normally refers to nonpositivity on the hyperplaneP
a ca = 0 [4]. The raw matrix M need not have that property when the transverse norms ra
differ. The correct statement is that a diagonal normalization is conditionally negative definite
and that M is congruent to it. This distinction is essential for standard terminology.
Let I = {a : ra > 0} be the active support. If ra = 0 , then za = 0 and the corresponding
row and column of M vanish. On I, define D = diag(ra) and
fM = D−1M D−1. (8)

Froggatt–Nielsen Power Ladders from Octonionic Associator
Geometry:
A Same-Frame No-Go and a Left–Right Bifundamental Realization
Non-Associative Field Theory Sandbox
July 9, 2026
Abstract
We test whether the observed Froggatt–Nielsen exponent pattern for quark masses and
mixings can be obtained from the locked octonion/G 2/E6 flavour mechanism. The desired
singular-value power laws are
md : ms : mb ∼ λ4 : λ2 : 1, m u : mc : mt ∼ λ8 : λ4 : 1,
with CKM off-diagonal hierarchy
|Vus| ∼ λ, |Vcb| ∼ λ2, |Vub| ∼ λ3.
For the same-frame squared-associator mass operator
Mab = ∥[ua, ub, H]∥2,
we prove a rigidity theorem: any exact geometric singular-value ladder is forced to be
(1, ϕ−1, ϕ−2),
where ϕ = (1 +
√
5)/2. Hence the same-frame operator cannot generate Cabibbo/Froggatt–
Nielsen powers. The minimal escape is the physically bifundamental left–right operator
Mab = ∥[ℓa, rb, H]∥2,
where left and right flavour 3-frames are independent. Numerically, this operator realizes the full
exponent structure simultaneously: down-sector powers (0, 2, 4), up-sector powers (0, 4, 8), and
CKM mixing powers (1, 2, 3), at machine precision for λ = 0 .2243. This is a positive existence
and mechanism result for the exponent pattern, but not yet a first-principles prediction of the
numerical Cabibbo value itself.
1 Locked octonionic input
Let O denote the octonions and let Im O ≃ R7 be the imaginary octonions with orthonormal basis
e1, . . . , e7. The associator is
[x, y, z] = ( xy)z − x(yz). (1)
In the locked algebra implementation,
[ei, ej, ek]ℓ = Aℓijk , A ijkℓ = −2Φijkℓ , (2)
1
where Φ = ∗φ is the G2-invariant coassociative 4-form. The verified component counts are
#{φijk ̸= 0} = 42, #{Φijkℓ ̸= 0} = 168, #{Aijkℓ ̸= 0} = 168. (3)
The contraction tensor
KDBC EGH = AF DBC AF EGH (4)
has 4032 non-zero entries in the full algebraic source.
Throughout, singular values are normalized by the largest singular value. Thus an FN ladder
is written as
sv(M )norm = (1, x, x2). (5)
For down-type quarks x = λ2, and for up-type quarks x = λ4.
2 Same-frame squared-associator operator
First consider a single orthonormal generation frame
U = (u1, u2, u3), u a ∈ Im O, ⟨ua, ub⟩ = δab, (6)
and a fixed Higgs/vacuum direction H ∈ Im O, taken in the numerical scans to be H = e7. Define
Mab(U ; H) = ∥[ua, ub, H]∥2. (7)
Proposition 1 (same-frame golden-ratio rigidity) . For the operator (7), the matrix M is real
symmetric, has zero diagonal, and has nonnegative off-diagonal entries. If its normalized singular
values form an exact geometric ladder (1, r, r2), then
r =
√
5 − 1
2 = ϕ−1. (8)
Consequently the only exact same-frame geometric ladder is
(1, ϕ−1, ϕ−2). (9)
Proof. Alternativity gives
[ua, ua, H] = 0 , (10)
so Maa = 0. Also
∥[ua, ub, H]∥2 = ∥[ub, ua, H]∥2, (11)
so M is symmetric, and every entry is a squared norm. Therefore
M =

The numerical rigidity scan confirmed this analytic result. Near-geometric random samples
with residual below 10−6 clustered in
r ∈ [0.6175868184, 0.6184807686], (17)
and the free geometric optimum was
(1, 0.6180339887498948, 0.3819660112501051), (18)
with zero geometric residual. By contrast, direct optimization to the Cabibbo bases failed:
x = λ2 = 0.05031049 : (1 , 0.5238896749, 0.4761103251), (19)
x = λ4 = 0.00253114540404 : (1 , 0.5012623691, 0.4987376309). (20)
Thus the same-frame route is structurally predictive, but predicts the wrong scale.
3 Left–right bifundamental operator
The preceding no-go relies on putting the same frame in both associator slots. The quark Yukawa
problem is naturally bifundamental: left-handed and right-handed flavour bases need not coincide.
We therefore define independent orthonormal left and right frames
L = (ℓ1, ℓ2, ℓ3), R = (r1, r2, r3), (21)
and the mass matrix
Mab(L, R; H) = ∥[ℓa, rb, H]∥2. (22)
This preserves the load-bearing octonionic associator but removes the condition Maa = 0 because
ℓa and ra are independent.
A random scan over independent L, R gave broad geometric bases rather than a rigid golden-
ratio locus:
r1 ∈ [0.0087411822, 0.6764874472], (23)
r2 ∈ [1.8132459 × 10−6, 0.3765974119]. (24)
Near-geometric samples were distributed over
r ∈ [0.0124376856, 0.4684062380] (25)
in the recorded scan, already showing that the golden-ratio constraint has been broken.
